top_signals.title: Treasuries Gain as Inflation Gauge Cools Fed Rate-Hike Bets
top_signals.summary: Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Oil Prices Swing as Traders Assess Pace of Hormuz Reopening
top_signals.summary: Oil fluctuated as traders closely monitored increased shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, trying to gauge how quickly supply constraints will ease after the US and Iran signed a preliminary peace accord.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Rise on Signs US Economy Is Holding Up: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street traders lifted stocks as signs the world’s largest economy is in good shape despite elevated inflation bolstered bets that Corporate America’s earnings will keep powering ahead.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Designing Drones for Africa
top_signals.summary:
This exclusive Cogs of War interview is with Maxwell Maduka, the co-founder and chief engineer of Terra Industries, an African defense technology company building autonomous drone and counter-drone systems designed for the continent’s operating conditions. As cheap imported airframes flood African markets and non-state actors employ drones across the Sahel, we asked Max why Terra is betting on Africa building its own defense industrial base.Cheap Turkish and Chinese drones and sensor systems have proliferated across Africa for years. Where do those systems fall short, and what capabilities do you hope to add to the crowded unmanned market?Imported Turkish and Chinese

top_signals.summary: Indonesia’s financial regulator will require influencers recommending crypto and other digital assets to hold relevant certifications as regulators worldwide tighten oversight of finfluencers.

alerts.title: Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
alerts.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.
alerts.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: New Foreign Direct Investment in the United States, 2025
alerts.summary: Expenditures by foreign direct investors to acquire, establish, or expand U.S. businesses totaled $232.2 billion in 2025, according to preliminary statistics released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Expenditures increased $76.8 billion, or 49.5 percent, from 2024 levels. As in previous years, acquisitions of existing U.S. businesses accounted for most of the expenditures. Acquisition expenditures in 2025 were $218.4 billion, expenditures to establish new U.S. businesses were $4.6 billion, and expenditures to expand existing foreign-owned businesses were $9.2 billion. Planned total expenditures, which include both first-year and planned future expenditures, were $284.5 billion. Full Text
